# Figure description: 18.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-II/18.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **Карта фактического материала** (Map of Factual/Actual Data) — a data-location or base-data map showing the spatial distribution of all survey data points, boreholes, and geophysical profiles collected during the survey. It is essentially an index or data-coverage map at 1:200,000 scale, serving as a master reference showing where all field observations were made.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers sheet **М-42-ІІ** (M-42-II) at a scale of **1:200,000**, situated in northern Kazakhstan. The map was produced in **1984** under the **Министерство геологии Казахской ССР** (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR) by the **Центрально-Казахстанское геолого-производственное объединение** (Central Kazakhstan Geological-Production Association). The coverage encompasses a significant portion of the M-42 quadrant, which is consistent with the broader survey area described in Report 36572.

A library stamp in the upper-centre of the map reads:
> **Казахская ССР Республиканский геологический фонд Инв. № [number]**

indicating the map was archived in the Republican Geological Fund of the Kazakh SSR.

---

## Key Content

The map displays the following primary data elements:

1. **Boreholes/Wells (скважины):** Numerous circular symbols (both filled/solid and open/hollow circles) are distributed across the map area. The hollow circles appear to represent deeper or more significant boreholes, while smaller solid dots likely represent shallower test holes or geophysical survey points. Borehole numbers are labeled next to each symbol, with prefixes such as **Оч-** (e.g., Оч-230, Оч-301, Оч-312, Оч-342, Оч-370, Оч-392, Оч-421, etc.) and standalone numbers (e.g., 7559, 7637, 7141, 7956, 3082, 2388, 4914, 7562, etc.). The "Оч-" prefix likely denotes a specific borehole series (possibly "Очаг" or a regional designation). There are also designations with prefixes such as **Д-** and **М-**.

2. **Seismic/Geophysical Profiles:** Prominent **dashed lines** cross the map in multiple directions — predominantly NW-SE and NE-SW orientations. These likely represent **seismic reflection or refraction profiles**, or possibly gravity/magnetic traverses. The lines intersect across the central and eastern portions of the map, forming a loose grid or network of traverse lines.

3. **Topographic/Structural Contours or Survey Boundaries:** Irregular **solid curved lines** appear in several areas (particularly southwestern and central portions), possibly representing geological boundaries, drainage networks, or survey corridor limits.

4. **Point data clusters:** Dense clusters of borehole/data points occur in the central, northwest, and southeast portions of the map, suggesting areas of intensive investigation — likely zones of mineral interest or structural complexity.

5. **No colour** is used — the map is entirely in black and white line work, consistent with Soviet-era reproduction methods.

6. **Legend reference:** A note at the bottom right states: *"Условные обозначения см. на чертеже №2"* — meaning the legend/key is provided on **Drawing No. 2** (a separate sheet), so symbol meanings must be cross-referenced there.
